svg.title: Tomasulo Out-of-Order RV32I CPU - block architecture
svg.desc: RV32I Tomasulo CPU mapped to rtl/top.sv. An 8-entry instruction-fetch queue feeds Issue/Decoder + Dispatch which renames sources via the 32-entry RAT, reads the 32x32 register file, and allocates one of 16 reorder-buffer entries plus one of 8 unified reservation-station slots. The RS dispatches a ready slot to the ALU (ADD/SUB/AND/OR/XOR) when opcode is R/I-type, or to the Branch Unit (BEQ/BNE/BLT/BGE/JAL/JALR) when opcode is B/J-type; the LSU is stubbed in this build (lsu_req tied to 0 in cdb_inst). The Common Data Bus arbitrates Branch > ALU > LSU and broadcasts {cdb_valid, cdb_tag, cdb_value} back to RS slots (snoop, fills Vj/Vk), to the RAT (clears matching renames), and to the ROB (writes value, marks ready). The ROB then commits in-order to the register file; mispredicted branches steer the PC and flush in-flight state.
svg.text: Tomasulo Out-of-Order RV32I CPU - block architecture
matches rtl/top.sv - 8-slot unified RS - 16-entry ROB - 32-entry RAT - ALU + Branch Unit (LSU stubbed) - Sky130 ASIC flow
register rename via RAT - allocate ROB tag + RS slot - read regfile
ROB head → RegFile commit_rd, commit_value (in-order, one per cycle)
arch reg → ROB tag - cleared by CDB on tag match
in-order commit - CDB writes value, sets ready
Qj, Qk + write rd → ROB tag
Reservation Station - 8 slots, unified (rtl/reservation_station.sv:38 logic [7:0] busy)
BEQ - BNE - BLT - BGE | JAL - JALR
dmem_addr/dmem_we wired at top.sv but not in flight
arbiter priority Branch > ALU > LSU broadcast {cdb_valid, cdb_tag, cdb_value} - every cycle, every snooper
CDB → RAT (clear renames on tag match)
